accept: Query simplified to the correct form: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type IN ('table'). FTS table is matched by name.
Test added: doctor index-schema check correctly detects all 5 expected tables (issues, issue_accept, issue_deps, issue_meta, fts) and fails when any one is missing.
Comment in doctor.go cites the SQLite virtual-table-in-sqlite_master quirk so a future reader doesn't restore the bug.
payload.description: Found by code reviewer 2026-05-10 at internal/cli/doctor.go:582-584. Query: 'SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type IN (\'table\',\'virtual\') OR type=\'table\''. The trailing 'OR type=table' is redundant. More important: virtual tables (the FTS5 index) appear in sqlite_master with type='table', not 'virtual'. The redundant clause masks that the IN list is wrong. Currently works only because of the redundancy; remove the OR and the FTS table check breaks.
payload.title: checkIndexSchema SQL query has redundant clause and fragile FTS detection
